dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Real-time overview of collector status and metrics.

metrics.empty: No metrics available. Click Refresh to load.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage collector configuration and debug tools.

unsaved.desc: You have unsaved changes. Leave without saving?

sonarStore.title: Sonar Store
sonarStore.desc: Configure the remote Sonar Store endpoint where metrics are pushed.
sonarStore.hostDesc: HTTP address of the sonar-store service.
sonarStore.stepDesc: How often metrics are collected, in seconds.
sonarStore.reportIntervalDesc: How often collected metrics are pushed to the store.
sonarStore.reqTimeoutDesc: HTTP request timeout when pushing metrics.
sonarStore.bufSizeDesc: Number of metric points to buffer before flushing.
sonarStore.channelSizeDesc: Internal channel buffer size for the metrics pipeline.
sonarStore.enabledDesc: Send collected metrics to the remote Sonar Store.
sonarStore.printMetricsDesc: Log every pushed metric point to stdout for debugging.

nodeExporter.title: Node Exporter
nodeExporter.desc: Collect host-level metrics: CPU, memory, disk, and network.
nodeExporter.enabledDesc: Collect and push node-level system metrics.
nodeExporter.labelsDesc: Static labels attached to all node metrics. Click a label to remove it.

processExporter.title: Process Exporter
processExporter.desc: Monitor specific processes by matching command-line patterns.
processExporter.enabledDesc: Collect and push process-level CPU and memory metrics.
processExporter.dynamicIntervalDesc: How often to rescan for matching processes. 0 disables rescanning.
processExporter.rulesDesc: Each rule matches processes by their command-line arguments.
processExporter.rulesEmpty: No rules configured. Add a rule to start monitoring processes.
processExporter.cmdlinesDesc: Comma-separated cmdline tokens. Prefix with ! to exclude (e.g. !seed).
processExporter.pidDesc: Directly specify a process ID instead of matching by cmdline.
processExporter.logPathPatternDesc: Regex to extract log file path from cmdline args (e.g. -LOG=(.+\.log)).
processExporter.extractTypeAutoDesc: Type not set. Both sep and pattern fields are shown. The backend will infer the mode.
processExporter.extractTypeDefaultDesc: Direct label assignment. No parsing needed — just map label keys to fixed string values. Useful for tagging all metrics from a process with a constant value.
processExporter.extractTypeSplitDesc: Split the cmdline argument by a separator (e.g. =) and reference parts by position ($1 = left, $2 = right). Example: --id=server001 with sep = → $2 is server001.
processExporter.extractTypeRegexDesc: Match the full cmdline with a regex and use capture groups ($1, $2…) as label values. Example: --id=(\w+) → $1 is server001. Most flexible, recommended.
processExporter.extractSepDesc: Character to split the argument on. Usually = for key=value args.
processExporter.extractPatternDesc: Regex applied to the full cmdline. Use (group) to capture values. Reference with $1, $2…
processExporter.extractLabelsDesc: Map label keys to capture groups ($1, $2…). Click a label to remove it.
processExporter.extractLabelsDefaultDesc: Map label keys to fixed string values.
processExporter.extractLabelsSplitDesc: Map label keys to split positions: $1 = first part, $2 = second part, etc.
processExporter.extractLabelsRegexDesc: Map label keys to capture groups: $1 = first group, $2 = second group, etc.

logConfig.title: Log Config
logConfig.desc: Watch log files and extract metrics using regex patterns.
logConfig.name: Config Name
logConfig.dynamicIntervalDesc: How often to rescan for matching processes, in seconds. 0 disables rescanning.
logConfig.metricHelpDesc: Prometheus metric help text. Optional description shown in /metrics.
logConfig.patternDesc: Regex to match log lines. Use (groups) to capture values or labels.
logConfig.metricValueDesc: Reference a capture group, e.g. $1 for the first group. Leave empty when using per-minute count mode.
logConfig.densityDesc: Minimum seconds between samples. 0 means no limit.
logConfig.minuteCountDesc: Count occurrences per minute instead of extracting a numeric value.
logConfig.timestampDesc: Capture group reference for timestamp, e.g. $2. Leave empty to use current time.
logConfig.timestampFormatDesc: Go time format, e.g. 2006-01-02 15:04:05. Leave empty to auto-detect.
logConfig.metricTimeZoneDesc: Time zone used for timestamp parsing, e.g. Asia/Shanghai. Defaults to system timezone.
logConfig.metricLabelsDesc: Static labels attached to this metric. Click a label to remove it.
logConfig.sourceSectionDesc: File path and process matching are mutually exclusive. Choose one source mode.
logConfig.filePathDesc: Absolute path to the log file. Supports * and ? wildcards, e.g. /var/log/game/*.log.
logConfig.rulesDesc: Match processes by cmdline to dynamically discover log file paths. Requires log_path_pattern on each rule.
logConfig.readModeDesc: tail reads new content from the end of the file (recommended). head reads from the beginning.
logConfig.encodingDesc: Log file encoding. Leave empty to default to UTF-8.
logConfig.maxFileSizeMbDesc: Files exceeding this size will be skipped. 0 means no limit.
logConfig.timeZoneDesc: Time zone for log timestamp parsing, e.g. Asia/Shanghai. Defaults to system timezone.
logConfig.watchPollIntervalDesc: File polling interval in Go duration format, e.g. 1s, 500ms.
logConfig.watchUseInotifyDesc: Enable Linux inotify for event-driven watching instead of polling. Linux only.
logConfig.watchRotateCheckIntervalDesc: How often to check if the log file has been rotated. Go duration format.
logConfig.watchMaxRetriesDesc: Max retries on file read errors. 0 means unlimited.

debug.title: Debug
debug.description: Regex debugger, process matching tests, and more.
debug.noResult: Click "Test" to run the debug tool.
